title: Design shared BLE advertising/service owner for native and Meshtastic app surfaces
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN-t2hn.4 intentionally made the Meshtastic BLE app-compat surface mutually exclusive with the existing native LICHEN NUS/SLIP BLE service so the first implementation has one advertising owner and one app session. This blocks simultaneous native direct-BLE LCI and Meshtastic compatibility over BLE on the same firmware image. What needs to be done: design a shared Zephyr BLE owner that composes advertising data/scan response, connection policy, CCC/session state, and service registration for native LICHEN BLE and Meshtastic BLE without double-starting advertising or corrupting per-client queues. Acceptance criteria: architecture is recorded in Beads design/notes; Kconfig policy for coexistence versus product modes is explicit; implementation Beads are created for code movement if needed; T-Deck/R1/R1 Neo assumptions remain below board/app config boundaries.
design: Shared BLE owner design recorded in docs/ble-app-surface-owner.md. Key decisions: keep current product modes mutually exclusive; a future ble_app_owner is the sole owner of bt_enable(), bt_le_adv_start()/stop(), and advertising restart; preserve CONFIG_BT_MAX_CONN=1 until per-client queues/CCC/read cursors/session epochs exist; keep native LICHEN and MeshCore mutually exclusive while both use NUS with incompatible payload semantics; Meshtastic/MeshCore/native surfaces retain payload validation and protocol queues behind owner-managed session hooks; board configs may keep native BLE defaults but app/product fragments select compatibility modes; physical mobile compatibility remains separate smoke-test evidence.
notes:
Input from project-LICHEN-9c4y.15: shared BLE advertising/service owner must account for three app surfaces: native LICHEN, Meshtastic, and MeshCore. MeshCore and current native BLE both use NUS UUIDs with incompatible payload semantics, so simultaneous native+MeshCore requires moving native LICHEN BLE to LICHEN-specific UUIDs or otherwise preventing clients from binding to the wrong service. First implementations should remain mutually exclusive product modes.
Completed no-hardware design work. Added docs/ble-app-surface-owner.md and linked it from docs/meshtastic-compat-dev.md and docs/meshcore-compat-dev.md. Created child Beads project-LICHEN-t2hn.22.1 through .22.5 for owner skeleton, native/Meshtastic/MeshCore migrations, and native BLE UUID coexistence decision. Checks: git diff --check passed. No Zephyr build required because this slice is docs/design plus Beads only. Reviews: Wegener found P1 Beads design-note gap and P2 board-config wording; fixed. Kierkegaard found P1 MeshCore PIN invariant plus P2 at-most-one/R1 Neo/board wording; fixed. Newton re-review and Singer re-review reported no remaining P0/P1/P2 findings. Commits pushed: 3f4faa35, 723a4062.
